title: Muallim Soniy
description: Arabic reading primer in Uzbek. Each lesson introduces Arabic letters and practice words with diacritical marks.
note: Lessons are separated by ٭٭٭ dividers in the original. Words are given with full vowel markings (harakat). The book covers madsiz (short vowel) words in lessons 1-12, then madli (long vowel) words from lesson 13 onward.

sections.title: Arabic Alphabet (Alifbo)
sections.description: Full Arabic alphabet presented in isolated form
sections.arabic: ا ب ت ث ج ح خ د ذ ر ز س ش ص ض ط ظ ع غ ف ق ك ل م ن و ه لا ي
sections.latin_reading: alif, ba, ta, tha, jim, ha, kha, dal, dhal, ra, zay, sin, shin, sad, dad, ta, dha, 'ayn, ghayn, fa, qaf, kaf, lam, mim, nun, waw, ha, lam-alif, ya

sections.title: Lesson: Alif (ا) — with fatha, kasra, damma

subsections.topic: Practice words with Alif and Ra

word_pairs.translation: he was attributed – he stood up straight
flower/bloom – back/appear – make appear – al-Azhar/most radiant

sections.title: Transition note: Madli words (long vowels)
sections.note_uzbek: Bungacha yozilgan arabiy so'zlar madsiz so'zlar edi. Endi bundan keyin arabiy so'zlarning madlilari keladi. Arabiy so'zlar madli bo'lganda zamma, fatha, kasra alomatlari ikkinchi turli yoziladi.
sections.note_english: Until now the Arabic words written were short-vowel words. From here onward the long-vowel Arabic words come. When Arabic words have long vowels, the damma, fatha, kasra marks are written in a second manner.
sections.content: All Arabic letters presented with long vowels (fatha+alif, kasra+ya, damma+waw)

sections.title: Lesson: More long vowel words (plurals and verb forms)
Lesson: Ya as long vowel (kasra+ya)
sections.letter_name: Ya (as long vowel, equivalent to ى)
sections.note: Ya and alif maqsura (ى) both read as long 'i' or long 'a' depending on context

sections.title: Lesson: Tashdid (doubled consonants) — Harflar
sections.note_uzbek: Ustidagi ushbu tashdid alomatlari quyilgan harflar ikki martadan aytib o'qiladi.
sections.note_english: Letters with the tashdid mark above are read/pronounced twice (doubled).

sections.title: Lesson: Mufaa'ala and Tafaa'ul verb forms
sections.note: Verb patterns with doubled and extended roots

sections.title: Lesson: Tanwin (nunation) — letters with tanwin
sections.note_uzbek: Tepasida yoki pastida ushbu tanvin alomatlaridan biri kelgan harflarda bir sukunli nun ortirib o'qiladi.
sections.note_english: Letters that have one of these tanwin marks above or below are read with an added silent-nun (an/in/un sound).

sections.title: Lesson: Alif and Hamza — nine forms
sections.note_uzbek: Alif va hamza to'qqiz turli yoziladi.
sections.note_english: Alif and hamza are written in nine different ways.

hamza_forms.description: Hamza on alif with fatha — initial or medial

sections.title: Lesson: Special reading rules — silent letters, alif maqsura, waw al-jam'

subsections.rule: Alif muqaddara — silent alif after fatha (implied elongation)
subsections.note_uzbek: Fatha alomatlari uzunicha aytib yozilgan fathali harflar yozilmagan alifi muqaddara ila cho'zib o'qulur.
subsections.note_english: Fatha marks written elongated indicate reading with an implied (unwritten) alif — stretched pronunciation.

subsections.rule: Ya maqsura — kasra elongation with ya
subsections.note_uzbek: Kasra alomatlari uzunicha aytib yozilgan hollarda cho'zib o'qulur.
subsections.note_english: Elongated kasra marks are read stretched (as long 'i').

subsections.note_uzbek: Zamma alomatlari vov aytib yozilgan harflarda cho'zib o'qulur.
subsections.note_english: Damma marks written with waw are read stretched (as long 'u').

subsections.rule: Ya read as Alif (alif maqsura at end)
subsections.note_uzbek: Ushbu so'zlarning oxiridagi yolari alif kabi o'qilur.
subsections.note_english: The final ya in these words is read like an alif (long 'a' sound).

subsections.rule: Waw al-jama'a — silent alif after plural waw
subsections.note_uzbek: Ushbu so'zlar kabi so'zlarning oxiridagi ko'plik vovlardan keyingi kelgan aliflar o'qilmaydi.
subsections.note_english: The alif after the plural waw in such words is not pronounced.

subsections.note_uzbek: Ushbu so'zlar kabi so'zlar o'rtasidagi aliflar o'qilmay qolur.
subsections.note_english: The alif in the middle of such words is silent/not read.

subsections.note_uzbek: Ushbu so'zlar kabi lomlar o'qilmay qolur.
subsections.note_english: The lam in such words (sun letters) is not pronounced.

sections.title: Lesson: Wasl — joining words in recitation
sections.note_uzbek: Vasl–ikki, uch yoki to'rt so'zni biriga qo'shib o'qishdir.
sections.note_english: Wasl means joining two, three, or four words together in reading. When two words are joined, one or two or three letters in the middle are dropped. The joining is recognized by an unwritten alif's fatha, kasra, damma, or sukun between two words.
sections.examples: Joining two or three words with elision of initial hamzat al-wasl

sections.title: Lesson: Waqf — stopping/pause in recitation
sections.note_uzbek: Vaqf–tinish degan so'z tinish to'xtov degan so'zdir. Qur'on o'qiganda dumoloq tinish alomatlari oldindagi so'zlarni o'qigach bir oz to'xtab tin olinadi.
sections.note_english: Waqf means pause/stop. When reciting Quran, after reading words before a round pause mark, one pauses briefly. At the pause, the word ending is either pronounced with sukun, alif elongation, or sukun+ya depending on the word.
waqf_rules: Round stop mark (ۚ or ۖ): pause briefly
At pause, final voweled consonant becomes saakin (sukun)
Ta marbuta (ة) at waqf is read as 'h' or silent

sections.title: Lesson: Mad — elongation (4 counts)
sections.note_uzbek: Mad–cho'zish demakdir. Ushbu so'zlarning ustilariga mad alomati yozilgan alif, yo, vov to'rt harf miqdoricha cho'zib o'qiladi.
sections.note_english: Mad means elongation/stretching. Alif, ya, and waw with the mad mark written above are read elongated by the measure of four letters.

sections.title: Lesson: Quran chapter opening letters (Huruf al-muqatta'at)
sections.note_uzbek: Quronda, suralarning boshlarida ushbu quyidagi harflar ismlari bilan o'qiladi.
sections.note_english: In the Quran, at the beginnings of chapters, the following letters are read by their letter names.

sections.title: Quran reading exercises — selected Suras (pages 31-43)
sections.note: The final pages contain numbered Quranic verses for reading practice. The verses are numbered within each surah and presented for reading aloud. These include suras from Juz' Amma (30th juz') commonly memorized by beginners.
